issues.description: The dedup is line-based (filtering lines that already exist in target). This could theoretically cause issues if a line appears in a different context (e.g., same text in different sections). However, for USER.md entries which are typically short single-line facts, this is acceptable.
issues.suggestedFix: Consider delimiter-based dedup for more robust matching in future iterations.

summary: Fix correctly addresses the bug identified in the core-script review. The original code detected duplicate lines but then wrote the raw source content instead of the merged content. The fix properly constructs mergedContent as existingContent + merge comment + newLines when merging with an existing target. The variable rename from `content` to `sourceContent` improves clarity. The merge comment '<!-- Merged from {source} -->' is a good practice for traceability. No new tests were added (only manual testing was done), but existing tests continue to pass. The suggestion about line-based dedup is a non-blocking improvement opportunity.
